annotator_1.text: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs).
annotator_1.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs)

annotator_1.text: The convolution operator's structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.
annotator_1.source_quote: However, its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

annotator_1.text: This paper presents a systematic taxonomy of operators that extend or replace the standard convolution in learning-based image processing pipelines.
annotator_1.source_quote: This paper presents a systematic taxonomy of operators that extend or replace the standard convolution in learning-based image processing pipelines.

annotator_1.text: The landscape of alternative operators is organised into five families.
annotator_1.source_quote: We organise the landscape of alternative operators into five families

annotator_1.text: Decomposition-based operators separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions.
annotator_1.source_quote: decomposition-based operators, which separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions

annotator_1.text: Adaptive weighted operators modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content.
annotator_1.source_quote: adaptive weighted operators, which modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content

annotator_1.text: Basis-adaptive operators optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights.
annotator_1.source_quote: basis-adaptive operators, which optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights

annotator_1.text: Integral and kernel operators generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels.
annotator_1.source_quote: integral and kernel operators, which generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels

annotator_1.text: Attention-based operators relax the locality assumption entirely.
annotator_1.source_quote: attention-based operators, which relax the locality assumption entirely

annotator_2.text: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs).
annotator_2.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs)

annotator_2.text: The convolution operator owes its fundamental role in CNNs to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.
annotator_2.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

annotator_2.text: The convolution operator has a structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator.
annotator_2.source_quote: its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator

annotator_2.text: The convolution operator's structure limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.
annotator_2.source_quote: However, its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

annotator_2.text: Alternative operators to standard convolution can be organized into five families.
annotator_2.source_quote: We organise the landscape of alternative operators into five families

annotator_2.text: Decomposition-based operators separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions.
annotator_2.source_quote: decomposition-based operators, which separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions

annotator_2.text: Adaptive weighted operators modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content.
annotator_2.source_quote: adaptive weighted operators, which modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content

annotator_2.text: Basis-adaptive operators optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights.
annotator_2.source_quote: basis-adaptive operators, which optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights

annotator_2.text: Integral and kernel operators generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels.
annotator_2.source_quote: integral and kernel operators, which generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels

annotator_2.text: Attention-based operators relax the locality assumption entirely.
annotator_2.source_quote: attention-based operators, which relax the locality assumption entirely

annotator_2.text: The paper provides a formal definition for each family of operators.
annotator_2.source_quote: For each family, we provide a formal definition

annotator_2.text: The paper provides a discussion of structural properties with respect to the convolution for each family of operators.
annotator_2.source_quote: For each family, we provide a formal definition, a discussion of its structural properties with respect to the convolution

annotator_2.text: The paper provides a critical analysis of the tasks for which each operator is most appropriate.
annotator_2.source_quote: and a critical analysis of the tasks for which the operator is most appropriate

annotator_2.text: The paper provides a comparative analysis of all operator families across the dimensions of linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks.
annotator_2.source_quote: We further provide a comparative analysis of all families across relevant dimensions -- linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks

annotator_2.text: The paper outlines open challenges and future directions for research on alternative operators to convolution.
annotator_2.source_quote: and outline the open challenges and future directions of this research area
